proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the 16 binary features (feature_010, feature_005, feature_001, feature_026, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_014, feature_009, feature_018, feature_008, feature_006, feature_032, feature_021, feature_030) is associated as a main effect with the binary outcome objective_response (univariate screen).

analyses.code: for each binary feature c, chi-square contingency table of c vs objective_response, and proportion-of-response difference (resp[c=1]-resp[c=0]).
analyses.result_summary: Strongest univariate associations: feature_014 +6.7 pp response (0.235 vs 0.167; chi2 p=5.8e-70); feature_001 +5.6 pp (0.221 vs 0.165; p=1.9e-55); feature_021 +5.4 pp (0.220 vs 0.166; p=6.6e-51). Smaller negative associations: feature_026 -2.3 pp (p=3.3e-6); feature_009 -2.4 pp (p=3.5e-5); feature_008 -0.7 pp (NS, p=0.14). Other binary features have NS univariate effects (p>0.3). Overall pooled response rate 0.1875.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ternary feature_004 (levels 0/1/2) is associated with objective_response, with higher levels predicting lower response.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_004')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency on the 3x2 table.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: feature_004=0: 23.1% (n=17592); =1: 17.4% (n=24971); =2: 12.9% (n=7437). Strong monotonic decrease (chi2 p=1.79e-90). feature_004=2 has the lowest response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the 18 continuous features is associated as a univariate logistic main effect with objective_response (sign of coefficient indicates direction).

analyses.code: Univariate Logit(objective_response ~ standardized_continuous_feature) for each of the 18 continuous features.
analyses.result_summary: Significant negative continuous predictors: feature_020 (b=-0.138, p=1.1e-30), feature_024 (b=-0.094, p=2.4e-16), feature_027 (b=-0.059, p=6e-6), feature_022 (b=-0.046, p=1.9e-4). Significant positive: feature_029 (b=+0.059, p=2.9e-7), feature_007 (b=+0.022, p=0.05). Other continuous features (feature_011, feature_012, feature_013, feature_017, feature_023, feature_028, feature_031, feature_033, feature_034, feature_035, feature_002, feature_003) NS at p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic model with all 35 features, the same set of features (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021, feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029, feature_026, feature_009) remain significantly associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response ~ all features, with feature_004 as dummies); standardize continuous.
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable estimates (log-OR): feature_004=2 -0.726 (p=5.5e-76), feature_014 +0.435 (p=1.7e-71), feature_001 +0.373 (p=2.4e-57), feature_021 +0.362 (p=8.1e-54), feature_004=1 -0.359 (p=1.9e-47), feature_020 -0.142 (p=2.7e-31), feature_024 -0.100 (p=1.1e-17), feature_029 +0.061 (p=1.4e-7), feature_026 -0.169 (p=6.1e-7), feature_009 -0.188 (p=3.6e-6), feature_027 -0.060 (p=5.8e-6), feature_022 -0.048 (p=1.3e-4). All other features NS. Adjustment changed nothing materially: features f001/f014/f021 are essentially independent of f004 and continuous covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three binary features with the strongest positive main effects (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) are mutually independent of each other and of feature_004 — consistent with random/independent assignment (e.g., either treatments or independent biomarkers) rather than a single underlying factor.

analyses.code: Pearson r and chi-square between feature_001/014/021 and feature_004 and each other.
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise r between feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 are all |r|<0.01 — no detectable association. None of these three is significantly distributed differently across feature_004 levels (all p>0.1). Marginal prevalences: f001=0.401, f014=0.300, f021=0.400. Distributions across feature_004 strata are flat (e.g., f001 is 0.402/0.403/0.394 in f004=0/1/2). Conclusion: these three features behave as if independently assigned.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exist pairwise multiplicative interactions among binary features for objective_response — specifically positive interactions between feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 (each pair amplifies the others' effect), and negative interactions between {feature_026, feature_009} and the trio.

analyses.code: For each unordered binary pair (i,j), Logit(y ~ i + j + i*j); record interaction coefficient and p-value.
analyses.result_summary: Top positive pairwise interactions all involve f001/f014/f021: feature_001:feature_014 b=+1.060 p=6.8e-103; feature_014:feature_021 b=+1.055 p=5.5e-102; feature_001:feature_021 b=+0.883 p=1.1e-78. Top negative: feature_026:feature_014 b=-0.604 p=1.1e-16; feature_001:feature_026 b=-0.375 p=3.3e-8; feature_014:feature_009 b=-0.468 p=6.8e-8; feature_009:feature_021 b=-0.409 p=4.7e-7; feature_026:feature_021 b=-0.306 p=6.0e-6; feature_001:feature_009 b=-0.281 p=5.0e-4. Other pairs of binary features either NS or weakly so.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a strong three-way positive interaction among feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 on objective_response: response rate is dramatically elevated only when all three are 1, with no meaningful effect of any one or pair alone.

analyses.result_summary: Triple-interaction coefficient = +2.126 (p=2e-91, z=20.4). Two-way and main-effect terms are all NS or weak after triple is in the model. Observed cell response rates: 7 of 8 binary combinations have response 0.16-0.17; the (f1=1,f14=1,f21=1) cell has response 0.633 (n=2401). Singletons: any one of {f1,f14,f21}=1 alone yields response ~0.16; any two of them =1 yields response ~0.16. Effect appears uniquely in the triple-positive corner.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1), feature_026=1 sharply suppresses the elevated response (negative modifier).

analyses.code: Stratify on triple-status; compare resp[feature_026=1] vs resp[feature_026=0] within triple+ vs triple-. Logit interaction triple*feature_026.
analyses.result_summary: Within triple+ (n=2401): feature_026=0 response 0.717 (n=2025); feature_026=1 response 0.184 (n=376) — absolute drop -53.3 pp. Outside triple (n=47599): feature_026=0 response 0.165; feature_026=1 response 0.167 — essentially zero effect. Logit triple*feature_026 interaction coefficient = -2.439 (p=1.8e-62). feature_026 is therefore a strong response-suppressing modifier specific to the triple-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1), feature_009=1 also sharply suppresses the elevated response (a second negative modifier, distinct from feature_026).

analyses.code: Same approach as h8 but for feature_009.
analyses.result_summary: Within triple+ (n=2401): feature_009=0 response 0.691 (n=2125); feature_009=1 response 0.188 (n=276) — absolute drop -50.2 pp. Outside triple: feature_009=0 response 0.165; feature_009=1 response 0.164 — no effect. Logit triple*feature_009 interaction coefficient = -2.259 (p=4.3e-42). feature_009 is a second triple-specific suppressor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full subgroup definition with elevated response is feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0; outside this subgroup the response rate is uniformly near baseline (~0.165).

analyses.code: Define subgrp = (f001==1 & f014==1 & f021==1 & f026==0 & f009==0); compare response rates and run chi-square.
analyses.result_summary: 5-way subgroup: n=1790 (3.6% of cohort), response=0.786. Complement (n=48210), response=0.165. Absolute response difference = +62.1 pp. chi-square p effectively 0; OR (unadjusted) ≈ 18.5. Stratified by feature_001/014/021 cell, the (1,1,1) cell with f026=0 & f009=0 has 0.786 response; all 7 other f1/f14/f21 cells (with f026=0 & f009=0) have 0.16-0.17 response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of the 5-way subgroup is robust across feature_004 levels (i.e., not driven by a particular prognostic stratum), although baseline response is lower at higher feature_004.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis: groupby(feature_004, subgrp).mean() and Logit(y ~ subgrp + f4_1 + f4_2 + subgrp*f4_1 + subgrp*f4_2).
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup response by feature_004: f004=0 -> 0.859 (n=617) vs 0.208 outside; f004=1 -> 0.772 (n=902) vs 0.152; f004=2 -> 0.668 (n=271) vs 0.109. Subgroup effect very large in every stratum; the subgroup x feature_004 interaction is modest (s_f4_1 b=-0.21 p=0.15; s_f4_2 b=-0.34 p=0.056). After adjustment for feature_004 and the strongest continuous predictors (f020, f024, f022, f027, f029), subgroup adjusted log-OR = +3.00 (OR ≈ 20.1; SE 0.060; p~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 5-way subgroup effect is not modified by any of the continuous features (no continuous biomarker further amplifies or attenuates the elevated response within the subgroup).

analyses.code: For each continuous feature c, Logit(y ~ subgrp + c_std + subgrp*c_std).
analyses.result_summary: No continuous feature shows a strongly significant subgroup interaction. Closest: feature_024 b=+0.126 p=0.032 (very mild positive interaction); all others p>0.1. Within-subgroup, response by tertile of any continuous feature is uniformly 0.76-0.81, confirming the absence of strong heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the refined 5-way subgroup itself, response rate is largely homogeneous across continuous features and other binary features (no further-narrow super-responder subgroup is hidden inside).

analyses.code: Within sub-cohort, response by tertile of each continuous feature; response by each remaining binary feature.
analyses.result_summary: Within 5-way subgroup (n=1790, response 0.786): tertiles of every continuous feature give response 0.76-0.81 (max range 0.76 to 0.82). For remaining binary features (feature_010, _005, _016, _025, _019, _015, _018, _008, _006, _032, _030), response stratified at 0.59-0.66 with no consistent direction. Conclusion: subgroup definition is essentially complete for binary features; no further refinement available from this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No three-feature binary combination other than (feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1) achieves a response rate above 0.45 in subgroups of size >=200.

analyses.code: Exhaustive enumeration: for every unordered triple of 16 binary features, every value combination (2^3 cells), filter to n>=200; record cells with response>0.45 or <0.05.
analyses.result_summary: Among 560 unordered triples * 8 = 4480 candidate cells with the binary features, only the cell (feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1) achieves response > 0.45 (n=2401, response 0.633). No triple gives response below 0.05 either. Confirms that the high-responder pattern is uniquely concentrated in the f001/f014/f021 corner.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_001 as a putative treatment, its effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients with feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (and is essentially null elsewhere).

analyses.code: Within mask = (f014==1 & f021==1 & f026==0 & f009==0): compare resp[f001=1] vs resp[f001=0]. In complement, same comparison.
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker subgroup (n=4579): feature_001=0 -> response 0.158 (n=2789); feature_001=1 -> response 0.786 (n=1790); absolute treatment effect +62.8 pp (p<1e-300, chi-square). Outside biomarker subgroup (n=45421): feature_001=0 response 0.166; feature_001=1 response 0.166; effect 0.000. Treatment-effect heterogeneity: the entire feature_001 effect is in the biomarker-defined subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_014 as a putative treatment, its effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (and is null elsewhere).

analyses.code: Within mask = (f001==1 & f021==1 & f026==0 & f009==0): compare resp[f014=1] vs resp[f014=0].
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker subgroup (n=6078): feature_014=0 response 0.166 (n=4288); feature_014=1 response 0.786 (n=1790); absolute treatment effect +62.0 pp. Outside biomarker subgroup (n=43922): feature_014=0 response 0.167 (n=30698); feature_014=1 response 0.160 (n=13224); effect -0.7 pp (NS). Same treatment-effect heterogeneity pattern as h15 — the high response is uniquely in the joint subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_021 as a putative treatment, its effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (and is null elsewhere).

analyses.code: Within mask = (f001==1 & f014==1 & f026==0 & f009==0): compare resp[f021=1] vs resp[f021=0].
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker subgroup (n=4531): feature_021=0 response 0.158 (n=2741); feature_021=1 response 0.786 (n=1790); absolute treatment effect +62.8 pp. Outside biomarker subgroup (n=45469): feature_021=0 response 0.167; feature_021=1 response 0.164; effect -0.3 pp (NS). The three framings (h15/h16/h17) are statistically interchangeable: any one of {f001, f014, f021} can be regarded as the treatment, with the other two plus f026=0 and f009=0 as the predictive biomarker pattern. Ground-truth identity of the treatment cannot be resolved from these data alone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_020 has a graded negative association with objective_response across deciles (no single sharp threshold; effect appears roughly monotonic).

analyses.code: Decile binning of feature_020 (range 0-23.6) and computing response rate per decile.
analyses.result_summary: Decile response rates (lowest -> highest of feature_020): 0.205, 0.202, 0.200, 0.188, 0.183, 0.180, 0.161, 0.143. Smooth monotonic decline of ~6 pp from low to high feature_020; no sharp threshold. Logistic main effect b=-0.138/SD (p=1.1e-30). Sign matches the multivariable model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (skewed, right-tailed: range 0.5-500, median 12) and feature_024 (range 20-100, median 60) are both negative continuous predictors of objective_response with monotonic trends (no clear breakpoint).

analyses.code: Decile response rates for feature_022 and feature_024.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 deciles (low->high): 0.210, 0.193, 0.192, 0.193, 0.186, 0.188, 0.183, 0.177, 0.181, 0.170 — gentle monotonic decline ~4 pp. feature_024 deciles: 0.210, 0.198, 0.209, 0.197, 0.187, 0.182, 0.191, 0.164, 0.175, 0.163 — ~5 pp decline; consistent with a continuous negative effect. Both effects are smaller than the binary-subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (range 18-92, possibly age-like) shows no main-effect association with objective_response; response is roughly flat across deciles.

analyses.code: Decile response rate for feature_011; logistic main effect.
analyses.result_summary: Decile response rates oscillate around 0.18-0.20 with no trend (low decile 0.180, top decile 0.185). Logistic main effect b=-0.002/SD (p=0.88). No detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 act as resistance markers in the responsive subgroup: each independently abolishes the elevated response in the triple-positive subgroup, and their effects are additive in the sense that either alone suffices to suppress response.

analyses.code: Within triple+ stratum, cross-tabulate feature_026 x feature_009 and look at response rate in each cell.
analyses.result_summary: Within triple+ (n=2401), response by (feature_026, feature_009): (0,0) -> 0.786 (n=1790); (0,1) -> 0.187 (n=235); (1,0) -> 0.182 (n=335); (1,1) -> 0.195 (n=41). Either feature being 1 collapses response to ~0.19 (essentially baseline). The two suppressors are not synergistic — either alone fully eliminates the high-response state. They thus behave as independent 'sufficient resistance' markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_004 and the strongest continuous predictors (feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029), the 5-way subgroup indicator retains an adjusted odds ratio above 15 — i.e., the subgroup effect is not explained by these covariates.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ subgrp + f4_1 + f4_2 + zf020 + zf024 + zf022 + zf027 + zf029).
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for 5-way subgroup = +3.00 (SE 0.060), OR = 20.1 (95% CI 17.9-22.7), p effectively 0. Adjustment did not attenuate the effect. Covariates retain their independent contributions (f4_1 b=-0.39, f4_2 b=-0.79, f020 b=-0.16, f024 b=-0.11, f022 b=-0.06, f027 b=-0.07, f029 b=+0.07; all p<1e-3). The subgroup effect is essentially orthogonal to these prognostic covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 contributes a graded prognostic effect on response that is independent of the 5-way subgroup: f004=2 < f004=1 < f004=0 in response, both inside and outside the subgroup.

analyses.code: Cross-tab feature_004 x subgrp on response.
analyses.result_summary: Response by feature_004 within subgrp=1: f004=0 0.859, f004=1 0.772, f004=2 0.668 (n=617/902/271). Within subgrp=0: f004=0 0.208, f004=1 0.152, f004=2 0.109. Both inside and outside the subgroup, response decreases monotonically with feature_004. The relative effect (subgroup - non-subgroup) is similar across feature_004 strata (~+55-65 pp).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest single-variable prognostic covariate among continuous features is feature_020, with a smooth, monotonic, dose-response-like negative relationship to objective_response that holds in both subgroup and non-subgroup populations.

analyses.code: Decile response rates for feature_020 within subgrp=0 vs subgrp=1.
analyses.result_summary: Within subgrp=0 (n=48210): low decile of f020 0.226 -> top decile 0.137 (~9 pp drop). Within subgrp=1 (n=1790): tertile response 0.813/0.800/0.744 (~7 pp drop, low to high). Effect of feature_020 is consistent in direction in both groups but not strongly modified — modest magnitude relative to the subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — There is a treatment-effect heterogeneity pattern in this AML cohort defined as follows: among patients with feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1, feature_026=0, and feature_009=0 (the responsive subgroup, n=1790, ~3.6% of cohort), objective_response rate is 0.786 vs ~0.165 in all other patients (absolute difference +62.1 pp; OR adjusted for feature_004 and continuous prognostics ~20). Equivalently, taking any one of {feature_001, feature_014, feature_021} as a putative treatment, that treatment's effect on response is ~+62 pp in patients with the other two markers positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, and ~0 elsewhere. feature_026 and feature_009 each independently behave as 'resistance markers' that abolish the response when present. Effect is robust across feature_004 strata, not modified by continuous features, and not explained by continuous prognostic adjustment.

analyses.code: Confirmation: subgrp = (f001==1 & f014==1 & f021==1 & f026==0 & f009==0); df.groupby(subgrp).objective_response.mean(); Logit(y ~ subgrp + covariates).
analyses.result_summary: Final confirmation: subgrp=1 response 0.786 (n=1790); subgrp=0 response 0.165 (n=48210); absolute diff +62.1 pp; chi-square p effectively 0; adjusted log-OR +3.00 (OR 20.1, p~0). Treatment-effect heterogeneity verified on this cohort.
